title: Cyprus Event Report: Copyright and Access in the Age of AI
content:
Blog post by Ypapanti (Dia) Kytta | CALIS

Greek version available here

On 11 May 2026, Knowledge Rights 21, the Foundation for Parliamentarism and Participatory Democracy of the House of Representatives of the Republic of Cyprus, and the Cyprus Association of Librarians and Information Scientists (CALIS) co-organised an event in Nicosia entitled “Copyright and Access in the Age of Artificial Intelligence.” The event focused on the challenges posed by the rapid development of AI for knowledge, cultural heritage, and rights of access to information, with particular emphasis on the Cypriot context.

The event brought together representatives from libraries, universities, research and cultural institutions, members of governmental committees on AI and the European Union, and legal experts specialising in intellectual property law.

Research and Protection of Culture in Cyprus: Challenges and Pitfalls of the Cypriot Copyright Legislation
